rationale: This provision addresses ad valorem tax exemptions for medical/biomedical manufacturer tangible personal property, unrelated to residence homestead exemptions. No conflict with the proposed homestead exemption increase.
quote: The legislature by general law may exempt from ad valorem taxation the tangible personal property held by a manufacturer of medical or biomedical products as a finished good or used in the manufacturing or processing of medical or biomedical products.
_note: Tex. Const. art. VIII, §1-x governs a distinct exemption category (medical/biomedical manufacturer property) and does not address residence homestead exemptions under Tax Code §11.13(b). The relevant constitutional authority for homestead exemptions is Tex. Const. art. VIII, §1-b, which is not the rule under review here.
